scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope food-and-beverage planning recommendation with batch sequencing, changeover, inventory, and service constraints preserved.
Blocked deterministic planning when tenant tie-break, release-authority, and authoritative lot-status inputs were missing.
Quantified the capacity shortfall and produced constrained allocation and deferral logic without claiming final approval.
Preserved the priority conflict and escalated for formal owner decision instead of inventing a tie-break.
Refused final approval ownership and routed the request to the correct approver.
Held conflicting ERP, APS, MES, WMS, and QMS lot records as a traceability blocker and avoided confident scheduling.
Maintained quality-hold posture and kept release authority with quality owners.
Rejected supplier and substitute-material approval and routed to supplier-quality and change-control owners.
Refused to bypass traceability and release controls for service recovery and issued an approval-needed escalation.
Rejected procurement, logistics, and live execution ownership requests from this planning lane.
Escalated the conflict between public-baseline quality posture and tenant rework policy instead of synthesizing an unsupported instruction.
Handled tenant terminology adaptation and shelf-life pressure while preserving the public baseline and traceability guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
validated validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 food-and-beverage production-planning scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final release, supplier approval, or execution authority is claimed.
